    "OccurredIn": [

    ],

About: It is about the re-adoption of monarchy in Brazil.
Affects: It affects the political landscape of Brazil.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Brazilian imperial family.
It is associated with the House of Braganza.
It is associated with the Federalist Revolution.
It is associated with the Brazilian Integralist Action.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the political movements in Brazil.
CausedBy: It is caused by the abolition of the monarchy in 1889 by a military coup d'état.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a series of uprisings and political acts.
CreatedTo: It was created to organize the country's monarchists.
FoundedBy: It was founded by Afonso Celso.
It was founded by the Viscount of Ouro Preto.
It was founded by Arlindo Veiga dos Santos.
It was founded by Plínio Salgado.
HasA: It has a history of uprisings and political acts.
It has a small movement in the present day.
It has a legislative suggestion for a referendum.
It has a legislative suggestion that reached 32,000 votes.
It has a legislative suggestion sent to the Commission of Human Rights.
It has a motion of support from Minas Gerais.
It has a motion of support from Rondônia.
It has a presence in military parades and similar events during Independence Day.
It has a visible emergence and growth throughout the country.
It has a legislative suggestion that was rejected by a legislative committee.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being fringe historically.
It is characterized by being a small movement to this day.
It is characterized by fragmented and peripheral acts.
It is characterized by the advocacy of restoration of the constitutional monarchy.
It is characterized by the support of the Brazilian Imperial Family.
It is characterized by being a minority and initially illegal under republican rule.
It is characterized by the nationalist and authoritarian ideas of the late 1920s and early 1930s.
It is characterized by a conservative political philosophy.
It is characterized by the support of Prince Pedro Henrique of Orléans-Braganza.
It is characterized by the support of Luiz Philippe of Orléans-Braganza.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the resignation of Pedro de Alcântara, Prince of Grão-Pará in 1908.
IsA: It is a fringe movement historically.
It is a small movement to this day.
It is a minority and initially illegal under republican rule.
KnownAs: It is known as the National "Bandeiraço" of Independence.
KnownFor: It is known for the emergence and growth of monarchist activity and organizations.
It is known for appearing with Brazil's imperial flag in military parades and similar events during Independence Day.
MemberOf: It is a member of liberal movements in the country.
OccurredAt: It occurred at Espírito Santo do Pinhal.
It occurred at Rio de Janeiro.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the Canudos War.
OccurredIn: It occurred in Rio de Janeiro.
It occurred in the community of Canudos.
It occurred in the state of São Paulo.
PartOf: It is part of the Federalist Revolution.
PerformedBy: It was performed by units of the Brazilian Navy.
It was performed by the Brazilian Army.
It was performed by members of a popular movement of socio-religious background.
It was performed by São Paulo monarchists.
It was performed by some Brazilian monarchists.
PublishedIn: It was published in the Gazeta da Tarde.
It was published in the Gazeta da Liberdade.
UsedFor: It is used for organizing the country's monarchists.
It is used for reporting directly with the exiled Brazilian Imperial Family.
It is used for expressing nationalist and authoritarian ideas.
It is used for establishing a new monarchy in Brazil.
It is used for raising public awareness of the issue.
It is used for discussing the possibility of a new plebiscite.
UsedIn: It is used in military parades.
It is used in similar events during Independence Day.
UsedTo: It is used to organize the country's monarchists.
It is used to report directly with the exiled Brazilian Imperial Family.
It is used to establish a new monarchy in Brazil.
It is used to topple then President Campos Sales.
It is used to raise public awareness of the issue.
It is used to discuss the possibility of a new plebiscite.
